commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And there happened to be there a man of Belial, whose name was Sheba, the son of Bichri, a Benjamite: and he blew a trumpet, and said, We have no part in David, neither have we inheritance in the son of Jesse: every man to his tents, O Israel. Septuagint, "lawless. The tribe of Benjamin continued to be rather disaffected. (Haydock) Part. This was an usual mode of expressing a disunion, 3 Kings xii. , and Acts viii. (Calmet) See chap. (Haydock) Seba was perhaps a kinsman of Saul, (Menochius) in some command.
